paper_title: Analysis of the error associated with the subsampling of Chironomidae in an intermittent stream of semi-arid Brazil

methods.description: Process for collecting chironomids from the Ipanema River and preparing samples for analysis.

steps.description: Chironomids were collected from three different sites on the Ipanema River, an intermittent river in semi-arid Brazil. Two sites were collected three times (April, July and October 2007) and one site was collected twice (April and July 2007).
steps.searchable_summary: collect chironomids from specified sites and times

steps.description: At each sampling, the sediment was taken using a D-shaped net (40 cm wide and 250 mm mesh). Since water flow was nil or low, the net was dragged for 40 cm at a depth of 2.5 cm.
steps.searchable_summary: use D-shaped net to collect sediment samples

steps.description: Samples were fixed in 4% formalin in the field and taken to the laboratory where they were preserved in 70% alcohol.
steps.searchable_summary: fix and preserve samples in formalin and alcohol

methods.description: Procedure for subsampling and sorting chironomids from collected samples for further analysis.

steps.description: At the laboratory, all Chironomidae on each sample were sorted and counted before proceeding to subsampling.
steps.searchable_summary: sort and count chironomids in each sample

steps.description: Subsampling was adapted from the fixed fraction and fixed count methods. The chironomid of a sample was homogenized on a gridded Petri dish with each square measuring 1x1 mm.
steps.searchable_summary: homogenize chironomids on gridded Petri dish

steps.description: Each column and line of the grid was numbered and then line and column numbers were randomly drawn. Individuals from the assigned 1 mm2 coordinates were selected.
steps.searchable_summary: randomly select individuals from grid coordinates

methods.description: Analysis of the error associated with the subsampling of Chironomidae, focusing on the correlation between sample size and error size.

steps.description: Comparisons were made between the proportion of larger (≥5mm) and smaller individuals (<5mm) between the samples and subsamples.
steps.searchable_summary: compare proportions of larger and smaller individuals

steps.description: It was established the correlation between the size of the error (difference in proportion of larger individuals between sample and subsample) and the size of the sample, using the rank coefficient of Spearman (a=0.05).
steps.searchable_summary: calculate Spearman rank correlation for error size and sample size
